feed.title: PLOS One
feed.rights: All PLOS articles are Open Access.

entry.title: C-terminal S-acylation governs membrane distribution, interaction dynamics and function of plant Rho GTPases
entry.content:
by Amir Akerman, Orit Gutman, Keren E. Shapira, Shaul Yalovsky

Rho of Plants (ROPs) are plant-specific Rho GTPases that regulate diverse cellular processes including cell polarity, vesicle trafficking, cytoskeletal dynamics and gene expression. Bacterial expression and biochemical assays demonstrated that S-acylation occurs at conserved cysteine residues in the C-terminal hypervariable region. Live-cell imaging of Arabidopsis seedlings revealed that S-acylation deficient ROP variants show reduced plasma membrane localization and altered interaction dynamics with downstream effectors. These findings establish post-translational lipidation as a key regulatory mechanism for plant Rho GTPase signaling.

entry.title: Spatial heterogeneity of microbial communities in subsurface marine sediments
entry.content:
by Jane Smith, John Doe

Subsurface marine sediments host diverse microbial communities that play important roles in global biogeochemical cycles. Here we present a high-resolution analysis of microbial diversity in sediments collected from multiple depths and locations across the Pacific Ocean. Using metagenomics and stable isotope probing, we demonstrate that microbial community composition varies significantly with depth and substrate availability. Our results provide new insights into the ecological factors structuring deep biosphere microbial communities.

entry.title: Editorial Note: Environmental justice and power plant emissions
